payload.title: Green
payload.content:
# Green

## The Perception of Green

Green, occupying the wavelength range of approximately 495–570 nm, is the color most abundant in the natural world. Human eyes possess peak sensitivity to green light, a trait thought to have evolved to help early primates distinguish ripe fruit among foliage.

## Cultural and Symbolic Dimensions

Across human history, green has carried a dual symbolism:
- **Life and renewal** – spring, vegetation, youth
- **Poison and envy** – the "green-eyed monster," green post-mortem discoloration

In many university traditions, green represents stewardship, sustainability, and the biological sciences.

## Spectral Science

Green is a primary color in the additive RGB color model (used in digital displays) and a secondary color in the subtractive CMYK model (used in printing). The eye's cone cells – L, M, and S types – respond most strongly to long, medium, and short wavelengths respectively; the M cone is tuned specifically to green.

## Applications in Academia

University curricula treat green as a case study in interdisciplinary analysis: physics (wave theory), biology (vision physiology), art history (pigment chronology), and linguistics (color term development across languages).

payload.title: Vault Summary
payload.content:
# Vault Summary

This vault contains notes on two primary domains:

## Architecture
Architecture notes covering coupling, reliability patterns, and flexibility principles.

## Colors
A new section focused on the perception, symbolism, and science of colors, beginning with **Green**.

The Green card has been placed into its dedicated note (`colors/green.md`) and into an architecture reliability note that deals with state and recovery – analogous to green of health.

_Last updated by CrashWeaver Stage 5 proposal._

operations.rationale: Creates a top-level vault summary note that serves as an overview of the vault's contents and organization.

payload.deleteReason: This note is an exact duplicate of architecture/coupling.md with no additional content. It is the least useful note in the vault as it provides zero new information.

operations.rationale: Removes the duplicate coupling note which adds no value beyond the original.

Inserts the Green card into an existing architecture note because green is commonly associated with healthy/operational states in reliability patterns, making it a related topic.
